items.title: Vast announces line of high-power satellite buses
items.snippet: WASHINGTON — Commercial space station developer Vast is moving into satellite manufacturing with a line of high-power satellite buses.

items.title: NATO jet shoots down Ukrainian drone over Estonia in escalation of airspace violations

items.title: Anderson confirmed as NASA deputy administrator
items.snippet: WASHINGTON — The U.S. Senate confirmed Matt Anderson on May 18 as NASA’s deputy administrator, the second-in-command of the space agency.

items.title: ESA-China SMILE mission lifts off to deliver first global images of Earth’s magnetosphere

items.title: European imaging companies step in to fill warzone gap

items.title: Four NASA payloads to fly on Astrolab’s first lunar rover
items.snippet: WASHINGTON — Astrolab’s first lunar rover will carry four NASA payloads on a mission planned to launch later this year.

items.title: US Air Force looks to convert offshore oil rigs into rocket recovery platforms

items.title: Virgin Galactic reaffirms plans to begin commercial service this year
